| genai AI applications : Learn different agent design paradigms | More agent (read: tool) options at your disposal to design genai solutions. Some current paradigms: <br>- Good ol' [Prompt engineering](https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/how-to-write-effective-prompts-for-ai-agents-using-langbase/)<br>- [RAG agent](https://weaviate.io/blog/what-is-agentic-rag), and [google Agentspace](https://cloud.google.com/products/agentspace?hl=en) agent<br> - sub-topic : [chunking RAG](https://stackoverflow.blog/2024/12/27/breaking-up-is-hard-to-do-chunking-in-rag-applications/)<br>- [ReAct (Reasoning + Acting)](https://interconnected.org/home/2023/03/16/singularity) Agents<br>- [Multi model agent](https://cobusgreyling.medium.com/multi-modal-agentic-applications-4a15ab0c0bf6)<br>- Deployment : [Serverless AI agent](https://langbase.com/) and [Managed AI Agent](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/agent-engine/overview)<br>- [Crawl4AI](https://github.com/unclecode/crawl4ai) - incorporate web scraping to provide context <br>- [MCP (Model Context Protocol) Servers](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/agents-and-tools/mcp) -- consolidated tools for Agents<br>- Building & publishing to [MCP registry](https://smithery.ai/) <br>- OpenAI [Function-Calling Framework](https://platform.openai.com/docs/assistants/tools/function-calling) <br>- Multi agent <br> - Libraries [google ADK](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/) or [lang chain](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain) <br> - DAG orchestrator [n8n](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n) | - Quickstart the adk. Build a sample agent using these as a starter <br> - [adk samples](https://github.com/google/adk-samples/tree/865332b356309a6cd59554830bd4a04d34d70dce) from the adk repo themselves<br> - [adk made simple](https://github.com/chongdashu/adk-made-simple) (youtube guide)<br><br><br><br>- [Prompt engineering course](https://www.deeplearning.ai/short-courses/chatgpt-prompt-engineering-for-developers/) , prompt engineering [whitepaper](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1AbaBYbEa_EbPelsT40-vj64L-2IwUJHy/edit) <br>- Sample [ReAct agent](https://github.com/TimoFlesch/agentic-llms.git)<br>- [Sample agents, multi agent](https://github.com/google/adk-samples/tree/main) from google adk<br><br>... since we're in a hype train right now can't really tell which course and paradigm gonna stay after this. <br>Here's a wise word from a developer that's gone through similar phase : ([reddit](https://www.reddit.com/r/Rag/comments/1fuc6c9/navigating_the_overwhelming_flood_of_new_genai/))<br><br>*I approached it the same way as I approached JS frameworks and libraries during the JS dark ages. I just went for the one that was made sense to me and was easy to start. When doing angular I felt there was to much for me to get, then react came out and I felt a little less like I had to use every single thing in the cabinet, but I could focus on smaller parts of the library at first. Then came personal projects and then a job. For these AI frameworks I started doing basic things with it like scraping websites and embeddings to talk to the pdf.* | FALSE |  |  | - Learn vertex [ML evaluation cloud path](https://partner.skills.google/paths/2311/course_templates/1080) to earn MLOps skills |  |

| RAG Pipeline Evaluation |  | Learn how to eval joon agents<br>- [google adk eval](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/evaluate/) <br>- [vertex eval service](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/models/evaluation-overview)[](https://google.github.io/adk-docs/evaluate/)<br> | TRUE | created a notebook to demo eval with vertex ai service<br><https://github.com/joon-solutions/joon-agents/pull/5> <br> | 30th Aug, 2025 |  |  |
